glsl/codegen

src/glsl/codegen.tur
defn

glsl-cat2

(glsl-cat2 [a :cstr b :cstr] :cstr)

concatenate two strings.

Parameters
afirst string
bsecond string
Returns

Concatenated string.

Example
(glsl-cat2 "vec3 " "pos")  ; => "vec3 pos"

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-cat3

(glsl-cat3 [a :cstr b :cstr c :cstr] :cstr)

concatenate three strings.

Parameters
afirst string
bsecond string
cthird string
Returns

Concatenated string.

Example
(glsl-cat3 "if (" cond ") {")

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-cat4

(glsl-cat4 [a :cstr b :cstr c :cstr d :cstr] :cstr)

concatenate four strings.

Parameters
a b c d -- strings to concatenate
Returns

Concatenated string.

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-sprintf1

(glsl-sprintf1 [fmt :cstr a :cstr] :cstr)

format a string with one substitution.

Parameters
fmtformat string with one %s
asubstitution value
Returns

Formatted string.

Example
(glsl-sprintf1 "return %s;" "x")  ; => "return x;"

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-sprintf2

(glsl-sprintf2 [fmt :cstr a :cstr b :cstr] :cstr)

format a string with two substitutions.

Parameters
fmtformat string with two %s placeholders
a b -- substitution values
Returns

Formatted string.

Example
(glsl-sprintf2 "%s = %s;" name value)

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-sprintf3

(glsl-sprintf3 [fmt :cstr a :cstr b :cstr c :cstr] :cstr)

format a string with three substitutions.

Parameters
fmtformat string with three %s placeholders
a b c -- substitution values
Returns

Formatted string.

Example
(glsl-sprintf3 "%s %s = %s;" type name init)

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-sprintf4

(glsl-sprintf4 [fmt :cstr a :cstr b :cstr c :cstr d :cstr] :cstr)

format a string with four substitutions.

Parameters
fmtformat string with four %s placeholders
a b c d -- substitution values
Returns

Formatted string.

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-int->str

(glsl-int->str [n :int] :cstr)

convert an integer to a decimal string.

Parameters
ninteger value
Returns

Decimal string representation.

Example
(glsl-int->str 42)  ; => "42"

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-join-vec

(glsl-join-vec [sep :cstr items :int] :cstr)

join a cons list of strings with a separator.

Parameters
sepseparator string
itemscons list of :cstr values (opaque :int handle)
sepseparator inserted between elements
itemsVec[cstr] of strings to concatenate
Returns

The joined string.

Example
(glsl-join-vec ", " (vec-of "float x" "vec3 n"))
  ; => "float x, vec3 n"

Since: v0.2.0

defn

glsl-join

(glsl-join [sep :cstr items :int] :cstr)
defn

glsl-indent

(glsl-indent [src :cstr] :cstr)

indent every line of src by 4 spaces.

Parameters
srcGLSL source fragment (may be multi-line)
Returns

Source with 4 spaces prepended to every line.

Example
(glsl-indent "return x;")  ; => "    return x;"

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-raw

(glsl-raw [src :cstr] :cstr)

return src unchanged.

Parameters
srcany string
Returns

src unchanged. Useful as a pass-through in higher-order contexts.

Since: v0.1.0

defn

glsl-stmts

(glsl-stmts [stmts :int] :cstr)

join a cons list of statement strings with newlines.

Parameters
stmtscons list of statement strings
Returns

Statements joined with "\n".

Example
(glsl-stmts (cons "float x = 1.0;" (cons "float y = 2.0;" 0)))
  ; => "float x = 1.0;\nfloat y = 2.0;"

Since: v0.1.0

defn

compile-glsl

(compile-glsl [src :cstr] :cstr)

return the assembled GLSL source string.

Parameters
srccomplete GLSL source string
Returns

src unchanged.

Example
(compile-glsl (glsl-vertex-shader "330 core" decls body))

Since: v0.1.0

Auto-generated by tools/gendocs.py. Do not edit by hand -- run just docs to regenerate.
